concept: **黄金矿工-可存档，复活，双人模式，联机对战:** Players control a miner whose claw swings continuously left and right; tapping the screen drops the claw to grab items. The claw retracts, pulling up gold nuggets, rocks, diamonds, or mystery bags, with heavier items like large gold nuggets ($500) taking longer to retrieve than lighter diamonds ($600). Each level presents a 60-second time limit to collect a specific monetary goal, starting at $500 for level 1 and increasing by $100 per level, with failure to meet the goal resulting in a loss. After successfully completing a level, players can spend earned money in a shop on single-use power-ups like dynamite to destroy unwanted items or strength potions to increase claw retraction speed by 25% for the next round. Progression involves clearing 10 unique levels, with diamonds appearing from level 2, before entering a cycle of increasingly difficult layouts, and a two-player local mode or online competitive mode allows players to race for the highest score within a shared time limit.
name: 黄金矿工-可存档，复活，双人模式，联机对战

concept: **Lep's World - Jump n Run Games:** Players control Lep, a leprechaun, navigating 2D platformer levels using on-screen left/right directional buttons for movement and a dedicated jump button for vertical traversal. The objective is to reach the end of each of the 160 levels, spread across 6 distinct world themes, while collecting gold coins and avoiding 9 types of challenging enemies like angry bees. Lep starts with 3 health points and loses one upon contact with an enemy or environmental hazard, but can restore one health point by collecting a cloverleaf power-up. Progression involves clearing levels sequentially, unlocking new worlds, and competing for high scores in a "Tournament mode" or directly against other players in "Multiplayer capability" modes.
name: Lep's World - Jump n Run Games

concept: **Worms Zone .io — Hungry Snake:** Players control a worm that continuously moves forward in a large 2D arena, steering its direction by dragging a finger across the screen. The primary objective is to grow the worm's length and mass by consuming scattered food pellets and the remains of other worms eliminated from the arena. Colliding the player's worm head with the body of any other worm results in immediate elimination, converting the player's worm into collectible food for others, while successfully encircling an opponent eliminates them and awards their collected mass. Various temporary power-ups, such as a speed boost (drains mass) or a magnet (attracts food), spawn periodically, and progression is measured by achieving the largest size on the live leaderboard and unlocking cosmetic worm skins.
name: Worms Zone .io — Hungry Snake

concept: **Jelly Shift - Obstacle Course:** Players control a continuously moving jelly character, swiping up or down on the screen to instantly change its vertical shape between tall/thin and short/wide forms. The core mechanic involves precisely matching the jelly's shape to fit through gaps in an endless series of static and moving obstacles like barriers and bars, without colliding. Each level features a unique obstacle course, with the objective being to reach the finish line in the shortest possible time while collecting diamonds. Successfully navigating multiple obstacles without collision triggers "jelly fever," a temporary speed boost that allows the jelly to complete the current level in approximately half the time. Progression involves clearing a huge number of levels, with 200 obstacle course variations across dozens of environments, and using collected diamonds to unlock new cosmetic jelly characters.
name: Jelly Shift - Obstacle Course

concept: **Bounce and Collect:** Players initiate gameplay by tapping the top of the screen to drop a single ball into a vertically scrolling, physics-driven board filled with pegs and multiplier gates. The ball falls and bounces dynamically, aiming to pass through multiplier gates (e.g., x2, x3, x5) that duplicate the ball count before landing in collection bins at the bottom, each assigned a specific point value. Progression occurs across distinct levels, each featuring unique peg layouts and multiplier placements, where players aim to achieve a target score or collect a minimum number of balls within a set number of drops, typically 3-5 per level. Successfully completing levels unlocks new board layouts and potentially upgrades like starting with 2 balls or increasing multiplier effectiveness by 10%. The game ends when all drops are used, with the objective being to maximize the final score by strategically aiming drops to hit the highest multipliers.
name: Bounce and collect

concept: **Coffee Stack:** Players control a vertically growing stack of coffee cups, moving it horizontally across a multi-lane track by swiping left or right to collect additional cups, coffee, milk, sleeves, and lids. The core loop involves navigating through gates and collection points to sequentially build a complete coffee drink stack, first collecting empty cups, then filling them with liquid, adding sleeves, and finally placing lids, while avoiding obstacles that deplete the stack by 1-3 items. At the end of each level, the accumulated stack of completed coffee drinks is automatically served to a line of customers, with each served drink granting coins based on its completeness and quality. Coins are used to unlock new cup designs, sleeve patterns, lid types, and permanent coffee shop upgrades that increase coin earnings by 5-10% or add new drink recipes, with difficulty scaling through longer tracks, faster movement, and more complex obstacle patterns.
name: Coffee Stack
developer: Rollic Games Oyun Yazilim ve Pazarlama Anonim Sirketi

concept: **Bridge Race:** Players control a character in a real-time race against 2-3 AI opponents, navigating a 3D obstacle course by swiping a finger across the screen to move. The primary objective is to collect colored blocks matching the player's assigned color, which are scattered across the ground, and then use these blocks to construct bridges over water gaps or climb ramps to reach higher platforms. Each bridge segment requires a specific number of blocks, typically 3-5, and players can temporarily hinder opponents by running into them to knock a few blocks from their inventory. Progression involves completing over 1000 levels, each featuring unique track layouts with varying obstacles like sliders and trampolines, and earning stars based on race completion time. Collected in-game currency allows players to unlock new character skins, block colors, and unique character animations, with difficulty increasing through more complex maps, faster opponents, and fewer available blocks.
name: Bridge Race

concept: **Snake VS Block:** Players control a snake composed of multiple balls, which continuously moves forward up an endless vertical track, steering horizontally by swiping left or right across the screen. The core mechanic involves navigating through a field of numbered blocks and collectible balls; colliding with a block reduces the snake's length by the block's displayed number (e.g., a '10' block removes 10 balls), while collecting small, unnumbered balls increases the snake's length by 1-5 units. The game features endless progression where the objective is to achieve the highest possible score by surviving as long as possible, with difficulty scaling as block numbers increase from 5 to 50+ and their spawn frequency and density intensify. The run immediately ends when the snake's total ball count reaches zero after hitting a block, emphasizing strategic movement to target lower-numbered blocks or collect enough balls to sustain hits.
name: Snake VS Block

concept: **Cannon Shot!** The game presents a 2D physics puzzle where players manipulate the environment to guide projectiles. Players use a tap-and-drag interface to position and rotate a limited set of redirecting objects, such as ramps, bumpers, or portals, within the level layout. Once positioned, the player taps a cannon to fire a fixed number of balls, typically 15-25, which then fall under gravity and interact with the placed objects. The primary objective is to guide all launched balls into 3-5 designated target buckets, with a level failing if the ball count in any required bucket is insufficient. Successfully completing a level unlocks the next in a linear sequence, and reaching specific level milestones (e.g., every 10 levels) unlocks new cosmetic cannon designs, increasing visual variety. Difficulty scales by introducing more intricate obstacle arrangements, increasing the number of required buckets, and demanding higher precision in object placement.
name: Cannon Shot!

concept: **Spill It!** Players engage in a physics-based puzzle by tapping anywhere on the top half of the screen to drop a limited number of balls, typically 3-5 per level, from a fixed or player-selected horizontal position. The dropped balls fall under gravity, interacting with various environmental elements and the primary targets: glasses, which are designed to be easily toppled. The core objective for each level is to knock over a specific number of glasses, usually 80-100% of those present, using the allotted balls, with a level failing if the target count is not met. Successfully completing levels progresses the player through a linear series of increasingly complex puzzles, introducing new obstacles and more intricate glass arrangements. Accumulating successful level completions unlocks new cosmetic ball skins and visual themes for the game environment, enhancing customization. Difficulty scales by increasing the number of glasses, introducing protective barriers, and demanding more precise ball drops to achieve the objective.
name: Spill It!

concept: **Long Neck Run** Players control a character automatically running forward on a linear obstacle course, using a swipe left/right or drag control to move horizontally across the path. The core mechanic involves collecting colored rings that match the character's current color to increase its neck length by +1 unit per ring, while collecting rings of the wrong color decreases neck length by -2 units. Progression through the course requires maintaining a minimum neck length to pass specific obstacles like zip lines (e.g., 15 units) or swimming pools (e.g., 10 units), with failure occurring if the neck is too short or the character falls off the path. Successfully completing courses awards keys and gemstones, with the final gemstone yield scaling proportionally to the character's final neck length (e.g., 1 gem per 5 neck units). Collected gemstones are spent in a shop to unlock new cosmetic character skins, such as a rabbit or ninja, offering visual customization. Difficulty scales by increasing the density and complexity of colored ring patterns, introducing more demanding neck-length requirements for obstacles, and increasing the character's base movement speed.
name: Long Neck Run

concept: **Mob Control** Players control a cannon at the bottom of a multi-lane battlefield, using a tap-and-hold or swipe gesture to aim and continuously fire small units, typically 5-10 per second, up the lanes. The core mechanic involves strategically aiming these units through various multiplying gates (e.g., x2, x3, +50 units) to rapidly increase mob size before they engage enemy units and attack the opponent's base. Players can also deploy special "Champion" units, unlocked via a card system, which possess higher health (e.g., 100 HP) and damage (e.g., 10 DPS) to break through enemy lines. The objective is to destroy the enemy's base (e.g., 500 HP) before they destroy the player's base, with battles ending upon base destruction. Winning battles earns "Championship Stars" which advance the player through a league system, and "booster packs" containing cards to unlock and upgrade unit types, increasing their stats by 5-10% per level. Difficulty scales by increasing enemy mob spawn rates, introducing stronger enemy unit types, and presenting more complex gate layouts that require precise aiming.
name: Mob Control

concept: **Square Bird - Flappy Chicken** Players control a square bird that automatically moves horizontally across a side-scrolling environment, with the primary interaction being a tap anywhere on the screen to make the bird lay a square egg and simultaneously jump upwards. Each tap adds one egg to a stack beneath the bird, increasing its height by 1 unit, which is crucial for clearing obstacles like varying height barriers (e.g., 3-5 units tall) or gaps. The game ends immediately if the bird collides with an obstacle or falls off the screen, with the objective being to achieve the highest possible distance score. Landing perfectly on the ground (i.e., touching the grass) exactly 3 consecutive times without hitting an obstacle activates "Square Bird Fever" mode for 5 seconds, granting temporary invulnerability and an increased score multiplier. Progression is centered on achieving higher distances and collecting in-game currency (e.g., "feathers") during runs, which can be spent to unlock new cosmetic bird skins. Difficulty scales by increasing the bird's horizontal speed and introducing more complex, tightly spaced obstacle patterns.
name: Square Bird - Flappy Chicken

concept: **Go Escape!**: Go Escape! is a physics-based platformer where players guide a ball through a series of increasingly complex levels by tapping the screen to initiate a jump of fixed height and distance. The ball automatically rolls forward along a predetermined path, and players must precisely time taps to jump over gaps between platforms, avoid static and moving obstacles, and land safely on subsequent platforms. Levels consist of 5-10 distinct platform segments, each introducing new challenges like vanishing platforms that disappear 0.5 seconds after contact or moving blocks that traverse the path every 2 seconds. Successfully navigating a level unlocks the next, with difficulty scaling through faster obstacle movements, narrower platforms, and more intricate jump sequences. The game features a single life per level; colliding with an obstacle or falling off a platform results in immediate failure and a restart of the current level.
name: Go Escape! - Casual Ball Games

concept: **Hide Ball: Brain Teaser Games**: Hide Ball is a physics-based logic puzzle game where players must protect a set number of "good balls" from "monster balls" by strategically manipulating the environment. Each level presents a static 2D arena containing 1-3 good balls, 1-2 monster balls, and various interactive elements like movable blocks, levers, and rotating platforms. Players interact by tapping and dragging specific environmental elements to create barriers or pathways, with each action consuming one "move" from a limited pool of 5-10 moves per level. After all player moves are expended, monster balls activate, moving along predetermined paths or seeking the closest good ball for 3-5 seconds. A level is won if all good balls remain untouched by monster balls after their activation phase, unlocking the next level; failure occurs if any good ball is contacted by a monster ball.
name: Hide Ball: Brain Teaser Games

concept: **Red Wrecker**: Red Wrecker is a physics-based puzzle game where players must remove all red shapes from the screen while ensuring all green shapes remain on the designated platform. Each level presents a static arrangement of various geometric shapes (red, green, and sometimes static grey blocks) balanced on platforms, and players interact by tapping on any shape to instantly remove it from play. The objective is to strategically tap and remove red shapes, causing the remaining shapes to react according to physics and gravity, ensuring no red shapes are left on screen and no green shapes fall off the designated area. Levels progress sequentially through 100 unique puzzles, with difficulty increasing via more intricate shape configurations, fewer available taps (e.g., 3-5 taps per level), or the introduction of new shape properties like explosive blocks. A level is won when all red shapes are gone and all green shapes are stable on the platform; failure occurs if any green shape falls off or any red shape remains.
name: Red Wrecker
